plan_analysis:
The question hinges on which individual the 88-member Assembly of Experts will formally elect as Supreme Leader before 1 Jan 2027.  Forecasters already know the constitutional framework, interim council, and a shortlist mentioned in mid-2025.  Still missing:

1. Current state: Who really leads in behind-the-scenes horse-trading right now; have dates been set for the Assembly’s special session; which blocs (hard-liners, IRGC, reformists, Qom clerics) control the Assembly.
2. Key drivers: a) IRGC preferences and ability to coerce/convince clerics, b) ideological leanings and patronage networks inside the Assembly, c) any “dark horse” compromise figures, d) public unrest pushing for abolition or a leadership council.
3. Background conditions & contrarian check: Likelihood the Islamic Republic collapses or the velayat-e faqih system is replaced; whether past debates on multi-member leadership provide a precedent; historical base rate of hereditary or “other” successions in theocratic systems.
4. Resolution mechanism details: Procedure, quorum, timeline, past behaviour of the Assembly in crises (1989), and whether constitutional amendments would be required for abolition.

The search strategy therefore gathers:
• Structural data on Assembly composition and faction strength (background/base rate).
• Historical precedents for Supreme Leader choice and any moves to abolish/alter the office (base rate/contrarian).
• Fresh reporting or leaks on current lobbying, endorsements, IRGC positioning, and scheduled Assembly meetings (current state & key drivers).
• Signals of regime-level instability or popular demands for ending the post (contrarian).

queries_planned.intent: membership list and factional/ideological breakdown to gauge voting blocs and base rates.
detailed account of the last transition to understand procedural precedent and timelines.

queries_planned.query: father to son succession theocracy examples
queries_planned.intent: historical frequency of hereditary handovers in comparable religious regimes; informs plausibility of Mojtaba Khamenei.

queries_planned.query: Provide analysis of historical proposals or attempts (1989-2026) to create a Leadership Council or abolish Iran’s Supreme Leader position, citing constitutional debates and clerical statements.
queries_planned.intent: map precedent and feasibility of “Position abolished/None” outcomes.

queries_planned.query: Assembly of Experts vote new leader 2026
queries_planned.intent: latest reporting on timing, special sessions, and frontrunner tallies.
gather recent coverage or leaks on IRGC endorsement, pressure, or opposition around Mojtaba.
track reformist/centrist clerical backing and public sentiment for Hassan Khomeini.
uncover reports of mass protests, opposition figures, or parliamentary moves that might lead to abolishing the Supreme Leader position.
